steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The crucial clue is the causal clause: “by lengthening the period in which they are providing financial support to their nonworking parents.” Extended years of supporting parents means less money available for personal wealth building.

The blank must describe how extended obligation harms wealth creation.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
”Stymie” means to block or prevent. If delaying transfers stymies wealth creation, the extended support obligation prevents workers from accumulating their own wealth—exactly what the causal clause establishes.

The other choices fail: compound and outstrip suggest wealth increases despite extended support (opposite of harm), while disparage describes criticism rather than financial impact.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
